title: Credential isolation modeled as a host-side policy/verify/guard/audit layer, not a host secret store
decision: Implement issue #19 as a new internal/credisolation package that codifies and verifies per-VM credential isolation invariants, plus a `nanofuse isolation verify` CLI command. Do NOT build a host-side per-VM secret store mounted into guests.

evidence.path_absent: /var/run/secrets/daax existed nowhere in the codebase at the time of this decision (this PR later introduces it as credisolation.GuestSecretsDir, the guard's protected path constant)
evidence.delivery_model: internal/firecracker/vsock_proxy.go + docs/specs/spiffe-integration-status.md show credentials are delivered guest-side over a per-VM vsock proxy to the SPIRE agent; the guest writes its own SVID locally
evidence.no_shared_mounts: Firecracker uses block devices + vsock; there is no host-path shared-mount mechanism into guests on main

alternatives_considered.option: Host-side per-VM secret store mounted into each guest
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Contradicts the existing vsock/SPIRE delivery model and issue requirement 'secrets cannot be mounted from host or a shared volume'; would create a parallel, conflicting credential path

rationale: Aligns with the established credential-delivery architecture; the genuinely-missing #19 work is enforcement and verification of isolation invariants, not a new storage mechanism

title: Runtime-enforced controls documented, not unit-tested
decision: The unit harness proves host-side invariants (0700 perms contract, cross-VM authorization denial, mount-guard rejection of host/shared bridges, SPIFFE identity distinctness, audit + terminate policy). Kernel-LSM denial of an in-guest cross-read and termination of a live Firecracker VM are runtime-enforced and explicitly out of scope for the unit harness (no root/LSM/running Firecracker on the dev box).

title: Self-contained new-files-only change to avoid PR #120 conflicts
decision: Keep all work in a new internal/credisolation package + new cmd/nanofuse/isolation.go (registered via its own init()). No edits to existing Go files.

context.overlap_risk: Open PR #120 touches vm.go, vm_handlers.go, types/vm.go, config.go, main.go — nearly every natural integration point

title: Harsh Codex review round (post-#137): fix mount-guard path byte-semantics, reject verifier-default change
decision: GuardMounts no longer strings.TrimSpace the mount Target: on Linux a mount target is a byte-string path where leading/trailing whitespace is significant (' /x' is relative, '/x ' is a distinct path). It now compares raw bytes — a leading-whitespace target fails closed with ErrInvalidMountTarget and a trailing-space sibling is correctly treated as a distinct path, not the store. The private-tmpfs exception was tightened to require an exactly-empty Source (whitespace no longer counts as empty), so it cannot be widened by smuggling whitespace. Added TestGuardMountsPreservesTargetByteSemantics and TestGuardMountsTmpfsExceptionIsNarrow and extended the fail-closed test.

findings.title: verifier exits 0 on NOT VERIFIED (absent store, non-strict)
findings.rationale: Deliberate, recorded design: a legitimately-absent store under non-strict is a lenient skip (exit 0) for CI health checks on hosts where the store is not provisioned yet; --strict already provides the exact fail-on-absent behavior requested (verified end-to-end). Flipping the default would contradict the prior Claude-review closeout and break TestRunIsolationVerifyAbsentStoreNonStrictExitsZero. The verifier never emits a false PASS.

findings.title: GuardMounts TrimSpace changes Linux path byte-semantics
findings.rationale: No bypass (trimming biased toward fail-closed), but incorrect path semantics; fixed by removing the trims and narrowing the tmpfs exception.

validation: mage ci green locally with CI-pinned golangci-lint v2.12.2 (0 lint issues; local v2.7.2 produced a stale-version prealloc false positive on the pre-existing internal/network/egress_test.go, unrelated to this change). go test -race green. CLI exercised end-to-end (PASS / FAIL / NOT VERIFIED / strict).
